author: qu0b
content: Whats the point of supporting multiple proof types in a single kurtosis network? Are validators expected to validate different proof types?

author: qu0b
content: Shouldn’t each kurtosis invocation have one global proof config?

author: barnabasbusa
content:
did you guys change something lately in ere? https://github.com/ethpandaops/eth-client-docker-image-builder/actions/runs/25255900272/job/74055167748#step:4:1 

imgs aren’t building anymore

author: taulepton_
content: yes they are. the idea is that we have proof diversity, you can consider this similar to client diversity.

author: taulepton_
content: There may have been some changes related to Hans PR above but I'm not certain of this

author: barnabasbusa
content:
https://github.com/ethpandaops/eth-client-docker-image-builder/blob/master/ere-server-zisk/build.sh

this is the build script we are using

author: barnabasbusa
content: i can see if any updates are required

author: taulepton_
content: I would defer to Han on this I’m not aware. Maybe the ere repo would shed some light

author: qu0b
content: Are these just dummy proofs? Or how is a local multi proof setup realised?

author: taulepton_
content: Both dummy and gpu. There is one service / container per zkvm prover (ere server) with gpus assigned to each service. Zkboost service acts as a middleware between client <-> ere provers with standardised interface

author: taulepton_
content: For dummy there is no ere service and zkboost produces dummy proofs itself

author: qu0b
content: So with 1 GPU 5090 i can run multiple prover backends?

author: taulepton_
content: I don’t think you can do partial assignment of gpu to a prover backends. You would need n gpus to serve n prover backends at the minimum. In practice if you want real time proving you will likely need more than 1 gpu per prover backend

author: taulepton_
content: I may be wrong about partial assignment as I haven’t tested it. However in practice I doubt we’d use it unless proof gen gets efficient enough to not require multiple gpus per prover backed

author: taulepton_
content: It should be noted that we want to produce different proof types in parallel

author: qu0b
content: So for locally testing multiple proof types I would need multiple GPUs. At that point id rather have proving as a service than running it locally

author: taulepton_
content: It’s likely proof providers will emerge. For those that want to operate their own infra they can do. Providers can censor, having an open source solution with easy deployment addresses the censor concern

author: taulepton_
content: Why do you want proving as a service for testing?

author: qu0b
content: I just want to better understand what the expectations are for clients that want to run this as we can’t expect everyone to have multiple GPUs to run a network locally but we can very well assume one GPU and we should focus not only on optimizing for our personal case but also keep in mind to make it as accessible as possible to others for testing

author: taulepton_
content: Yep that was the ambition with the Kurtosis/Ethereum package integration. There are two “beacon node types” proof generating nodes and proof verifying nodes. Multiple beacon nodes can connect to a single zkboost proof generator/verifier backend. If you don’t have a gpu then run with mock mode set on zkboost in the params file. Open to suggestions on how we can improve this.

author: taulepton_
content: We had designed this to be generic but it’s possible there’s use cases we don’t cover. Keen to collaborate further with pandaops to surface and unlock these use cases.

author: chotuchaiwala
content: Thanks for this, just to be clear, the single zkboost instance still needs a dedicated ere-server and GPU(s) for respective proof type, right?

author: chotuchaiwala
content: In ere proofs*

author: taulepton_
content: Yes that’s correct, if you want to generate real proofs. If you are happy to use mock proofs then you only need zkboost - no ere required.

author: chotuchaiwala
content: Thanks 🙏

author: taulepton_
content: ere server = zkvm prover backend. Proof type = prover backend + guest type. I.e Reth-zisk = zisk zkvm + reth guest program. ethrex-zisk = zisk zkvm + ethrex guest.

author: taulepton_
content: So in practice you can have multiple proof types from a single ere backend

author: taulepton_
content: Just different guest programs

author: chotuchaiwala
content: Can we swap the EL guest program during runtime?

author: taulepton_
content: Would be good to determine some concrete test cases, metrics and auxiliary information we would like to observe / assert so we can build with this in mind

author: taulepton_
content: You can specify the guest via the api request argument

author: taulepton_
content: But you have to specify the guest at startup such that the circuit can be downloaded

author: chotuchaiwala
content: So for example: no zisk-reth -> zisk-ethrex swap once the ere server is up and running?

author: taulepton_
content: You specify support for both of them at startup so that both the circuits can be fetched. Then for each proof request you specify which guest to use.

author: taulepton_
content: If you are having trouble operating the stack / it’s not easy to use please let me know of the challenges you’re experiencing so we can get them resolved.

author: chotuchaiwala
content: No troubles yet, just making sure I am getting things right. The examples have been quite helpful.

author: taulepton_
content: Based on our discussions it seems like maybe we’re lacking some documentation that digs into the details we have discussed here?

author: chotuchaiwala
content: Agree on this

author: chotuchaiwala
content: If you want I can draft things up and you can review them

author: taulepton_

author: chotuchaiwala
content: Honestly, I have no idea where exactly we keep our docs

author: taulepton_
content: I think we can add some additional documentation to the etheruem package

author: chotuchaiwala
content: Sounds good to me, lets see if others feel the same

author: taulepton_
content: I’m keen to assist with pushing this effort forward so let me know what your plans/thoughts are and we can discuss how we can assist to support progress

author: chotuchaiwala
content: Sure, lets do it
